title: Forwards (Advanced): The value of a short forward contract is always positive...
summary: Distinguish futures from forward contracts based on standardization and exchange features.
prompt: The value of a short forward contract is always positive when the spot price increases.
solution: This statement is false. The value of a short forward contract decreases when the spot price increases, as the trader is now obligated to sell at a lower predetermined price compared to the current spot price. This relationship is crucial for understanding the implications of market movements on forward contracts.
